evidence: The paper uses Random Forest impurity importance (via Ranger package) to rank features and states: "Among the 18 features retained after the feature selection process, the average temperature and pressure in February (T2M_2 and PS_2), as well as the average wind speed in January (WS2M_1), emerged as the most significant predictors" (Sect. 3.4). Figure 2 displays "Feature importance based on Random Forest" with explicit ranking. No permutation importance, SHAP, PDP/ALE, or held-out validation of importance is provided. Boruta is used for feature selection (zero/non-zero detection) but not to validate the ranking. Discussion and conclusions hinge on this ranking: "Beekeepers can use pre-harvest weather parameters, such as winter temperature, humidity, atmospheric pressure, and wind speed, to inform management strategies" (Conclusions). Dataset contains multiple correlated monthly variants (T2M_12, T2M_1, T2M_2, T2M_MAX_1/12/2, T2M_MIN_1/12/2; RH2M variants; WS2M variants in different months), EVI at 5 different months, and features spanning disparate scales (pressure ~99 kPa, temperature ~2-15°C, wind ~1 m/s, EVI ~0.4-0.55).
